title: Ixeridium yakuinsulare
taxonomy: Ixeridium yakuinsulare is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Japan. The exact classification of Ixeridium yakuinsulare is as follows: the genus Ixeridium is within the tribe Senecioneae. Within the family Asteraceae, the order of classification is: Asteraceae > Senecioneae > Ixeridium > Ixeridium yakuinsulare. Further classification includes kingdom: Plantae, phylum: Magnoliophyta, class: Magnoliopsida, and other subcategories.

intro: Ixeridium yakuinsulare, also known as Yaku Island Ixeris, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Yaku Island in Japan. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of Ixeridium yakuinsulare include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Japanese medicine, Ixeridium yakuinsulare is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
